text.title: shadcn/ui Antipatterns
text.summary: Hallucinated imports, use-client over-application, inline colors, forwardRef loss, and Storybook setup gaps.

antipattern.description: Importing shadcn components from `@shadcn/ui/button` (npm-style) instead of the local `@/components/ui/button`.
antipattern.reason: There is no `@shadcn/ui` npm package. shadcn is copied into the codebase. The correct import is always the local alias configured in `tsconfig.json` paths (`@/` → `src/`).
Inline Tailwind colors bypass the theme system, breaking dark mode and brand customization. Use the CVA variant system; add a new variant if the existing ones don't cover the case.

antipattern.description: Adding `'use client'` to a page or layout to use a Button component.
antipattern.reason: This marks the entire subtree as client-rendered, eliminating RSC benefits for all server data fetching. Move the `'use client'` to the smallest leaf component that actually needs state.

antipattern.description: Removing `React.forwardRef` from a primitive component during refactoring.
antipattern.reason: Radix's `asChild` pattern requires refs to be forwarded. Removing `forwardRef` breaks portal-based components (Dialog, Popover, Tooltip) in subtle ways that only surface with specific composition patterns.

antipattern.description: Running `npx shadcn add button --yes` without checking for local customizations first.
antipattern.reason: Re-running `add` overwrites local edits. Always run `npx shadcn diff` first to review upstream changes; then manually merge the relevant parts.
Without the globals import, Storybook renders all shadcn components unstyled — CSS variables and Tailwind base styles are missing.

antipattern.description: Dynamic Tailwind class strings like `bg-${color}-500` without a safelist entry.
antipattern.reason: Tailwind's JIT compiler purges classes not found as complete strings in source files. Dynamic class construction produces classes that are purged at build time, rendering unstyled in production.
